def: s.f. = S کشمانڐ कुष्माण्ड kushmāṇḍ, s.m. A kind of pumpkin-gourd, Benincasa cerifera; a drug; — (kushmāṇḍ) a kind of imp, one of a class of demi-gods attached to Śiva; a particular religious formula, or sacred text; a certain rite performed as a penance or expiation.

ety: v.n. fr. kushtan; rt. Zend kush = S. कुष्
def: s.f. Killing, slaughter
part. Killed, slain; — s.m. One who is killed or slain, a victim; (poet.) a lover; — killed mercury, a preparation of mercury; calcined metal, calx.
s.m. pl. (of kushta), The killed, the slain; (poet.) those who are desperately in love.
adj. Fit to be killed, deserving or worthy of death; to be killed, destined for slaughter.
s.m. A sort of medicinal plant, Costus speciosus or arabicus; — leprosy
subs: s.m. Name of several plants which cure leprosy; white mustard; a sort of yam, the root of Dioscorea

def: adj. & s.m. Leprous; — a leper.
adj. & s.m. Leprous; — a leper.
s.f. Wrestling, struggling
subs: or kushtī-gar, or kushtī-gīr, s.m. A wrestler
kushtī khelnā, or kushtī laṛnā (-se), To wrestle, struggle.

ety: v.n. of kushūdan = kushādan; see kushā
def: s.f. Opening, expanding; openness, expansion; expanding or dilating with joy (the heart, &c.); — (contract. of kushūda), perf. part. Opened, expanded; open.
s.m.(?), A large body (of troops), an army; a body, company.
s.m. (f. -inī), A person holding the kuśa grass.
s.m. Any loan or thing lent to be repaid with interest; usury; the profession of usury; — (f. -ā), Money-lender ( = kusīdā).
s.f. = kusīdin, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ī), Money-lender, usurer.
s.m. = kusīdā.
s.f. = kusīdin.
s.m. A usurer ( = kusīdā, and kusīd).
s.f. The wife of a usurer; a female money-lender.
s.f. The wife of a usurer; a female money-lender.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Ill-behaved, rude; ill-tempered; evil-disposed, wicked; — an evil-disposed person, &c.
s.f. = S کشيلتو कुशीलत्व kuśīlatva, s.m. Misbehaviour, misconduct; bad disposition; ill-temper.
s.m. = S کشيلتا कुशीलता kuśīlatā, s.f. Misbehaviour, misconduct; bad disposition; ill-temper.
s.m. An actor, dancer, mime; newsmonger; — the two sons of Rāma, Kuśa and Lava (who recited the Rāmāyaṇa).
part. = P کسستگي kusistagī, s.f. See gusistagī; and gusista.
s.f. = P کسسته kusista, part. See gusistagī; and gusista.

ety: S. कृष्+इका, or dim. of kussā
def: s.f. A ploughshare; a hoe; a small mattock.
s.f. See s.v. kus.
s.m. A mattock, a pickaxe (syn. phālī).
s.f.(?), The plant Costus arabicus.
ex: گُزِشْتَہ قِسْط
guziśtā qist
previous episode
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قِسْط (qist), borrowed from Arabic قِسْط (qisṭ).

def: s.m. (dialec.), The waist-cloth corresponding to the dhotī (q.v.), worn by the women of Bundelkhanḍ (it is fuller than the dhotī; but leaves the greater part of the legs bare)
s.f. corr. of kushtī, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of کسف 'to be obscured,' &c.
def: s.m. Eclipse (of the sun).
s.m. A flower; fruit; — the menstrual discharge (syn. phūl); — ophthalmia
subs: (˚ma+an˚), s.m. Calx of brass (used as a collyrium)
(˚ma+āy˚), s.m. lit. 'Flower-armed'; an epithet of Kāma, the god of love, whose arrows are tipped with flowers)
Disorder of the menses, excessive menstrual discharge.

def: s.m. Bastard saffron, safflower, Carthamus tinctorius (from the flower of which a red dye is made); the red dye of safflower; — the water-pot of a theological student, or of a sannyāsī; — gold.
s.m. = کسمبهہ kusumbh, q.v.
= kusumbhā, q.v.
s.m. Safflower (see kusum).
s.m. = کسمبهہ kusumbh, q.v.
s.m. Safflower ( = kusum); the dye of safflower; (met.) an infusion of bhaṅg, q.v. (used for intoxication); — adj. (f. -ī), Coloured or dyed with safflower, red.
= H کسنبهي kusumbhī, See کسمبها kusumbhā; and کسمبهي kusumbhī.
= H کسنبها kusumbhā, See کسمبها kusumbhā; and کسمبهي kusumbhī.
s.f. Cloth dyed with safflower; — scarlatina; measles.
s.f. Cloth dyed with safflower; — scarlatina; measles.
s.f. The dye of safflower.
part. adj. Furnished with flowers; flowering, in flower budded; full-blown; ripe.
s.m. Any very severe misfortune.

ety: inf. n. of قصر; see qaṣr
def: s.m. A falling short (of), a failing (of or in); deficiency; decrease; defect; failure, want, default; omission; miss; shortcoming, error, faultiness, fault, sin; inaccuracy, incorrectness; — the allowance or premium on the exchange of rupees (in contradistinction to baṭṭā or discount)
subs: (-kā), To point out the fault (of); to point out faults (in, -meṅ), to find fault (with)
v.n. To fall short (of, -se), to fail (of, -se, or in, -meṅ); to omit; to neglect; to miss (a mark); to be deficient; to be wanting (in); to commit a fault; to withhold (from)
or quṣūr-mand, adj. At fault, to blame, blameworthy, &c.
adj. & adv. Without defect or fault; guiltless, innocent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُصُور (qusūr), from Arabic قُصُور (quṣūr).

def: s.m. pl. (of qaṣr, q.v.), Castles; palaces; villas, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُصُور (qusūr), from Arabic قُصُور (quṣūr).

def: s.m. Decimal fractions
s.m. An evil disposition.
s.m. (f. -inī), A person of an evil disposition.
v.t. To cause to be cursed, &c.
s.m. A bad dream, nightmare; unsound sleep.

ety: inf. n. of قوت 'to feed, to nourish,' &c.
def: s.f. Food, aliment, nutriment, victuals, provisions, sustenance, livelihood
subs: Food sufficient to keep body and soul together
To manage to live, to exist.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُوت (qūt), from Arabic قُوت (qūt).

def: s.m. The root of the Costus speciosus or Arabicus (it is used medicinally).
s.m. A survey; task; valuation, appraisement, a rough estimate; conjecture, guess.
s.m. The base of a right-angled triangle.
s.m. Macerated paper, paper used for making paste-board, papier-maché.
s.f. 'Pounded paper,' papier-mache (see kuṭṭī), stiffened paper (inserted between the cloth and lining of caps, &c.).
s.m. A valuer, an appraiser.

s.m. A prominence, projection; summit or peak (of a mountain), summit, top, head; the highest, the most excellent, the first; — a heap; a multitude; — an anvil; a hammer, a mallet; a trap (for catching deer, &c.); — an enigma; a puzzling question; a knotty point; an obscure passage or stanza; — a confederacy, combination, league; a concerted scheme or plot; — s.f. Mimicry, buffoonery; — illusion, fraud, trick, deceit; falsehood; — adj. False, untrue
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Situated at the summit, standing at the top, keeping the highest position; — immovable, uniform, unchanging, unalterable, perpetually and universally the same (as the Deity, the soul, space, &c.); — a chief; the head or common spring (of families now divergent).

def: (rustic) = kūṭā, perf. part. of kūṭnā, q.v.
s.f. contrac. of kotāhī, q.v.
s.f. Knocking, hammering, pounding, bruising; a blow; — price paid for pounding or bruising.
s.m. The medicinal plant Wrightia (or Echites) antidysenterica (the seeds of which are used as a vermifuge).
s.m. One of the rules of Arithmetic or Algebra; the rule of Position.
s.m. A club; a pestle; a short stick with which bhang is ground
subs: To receive a cudgelling or beating
(-ko), To cudgel, to thrash with a stick.

def: s.m. = کوتك kūtak, q.v.
s.m. The tree Calosanthes indica.
s.m. The eighth muhurta or portion of the day from the last daṇḍa of the second watch to the first of the third, noon-tide (nearly); — a sort of blanket made of the hair of the mountain-goat.
s.m. A poor, or miserable, horse; a hack; — an impatient little horse.
s.m. Wrong reasoning, fallacious argument; false doctrine; insignificance in judgment or in discernment.
s.f. Illogical or fallacious reasoning.
v.t. To cut with the teeth, to gnaw, to nibble; to peck
subs: part. adj. (f. -ī), Gnawed, nibbled; incised; bitten; pecked; pitted (with small-pox)

def: v.t. To cause to be gnawed, or bitten, &c.
s.f. = S کوٿتو कूटत्व kūṭatva, s.m. Mimicry, buffoonery, &c. ( = kūṭ, q.v.).
s.m. = S کوٿتا कूटता kūṭatā, s.f. Mimicry, buffoonery, &c. ( = kūṭ, q.v.).

ety: v.n. fr. قطب 'to collect, to draw together,' &c.
def: s.m. The polar star, the north pole; — a centre round which anything revolves; the iron spindle of a millstone; an axis; a pivot; — a prince, lord, chief; — a title or degree of rank among religious mendicants
subs: s.m. The antartic or south pole
or qutb-ě-samāwī, The axis of heaven; the celestial pole

def: s.m. The two poles.
adj. Of or relating to the north pole; polar
s.f. A bad habit.
s.m. A low place.
s.m. The woodpecker, Picus.
s.f. = kuṭhārī, q.v.
s.m. An axe, a hatchet; a sort of hoe or spade; — a tree.
s.m. The post round which the string of the churning stick winds.
s.f. A small axe or hatchet ( = kulhāṛī, q.v.); — a crucible, an earthen pot or vase for melting gold, or silver, &c.
s.m. Wrong place; bad place; — adj. Misplaced, out of place.
s.f. Bad place; wrong place; — adj. Out of place, misplaced.
s.m. A large earthen vessel in which grain is kept, a small earthen granary, a corn-bin (syn. kuṭhlā).

ety: kuṭhiyā, q.v. + ar = Prk. अडो = S. रः
def: s.m. A store-house, granary ( = kuṭhlā; koṭhṛī).

ety: kuṭhiyār + ī = Prk. इओ = S. इकः (इन्+कः)
def: s.m. One who is in charge of a store-house or granary, a store-keeper.
s.m. (rustic) A small earthen granary, a corn-bin; a store-house (cf. kothṛī); a lime-kiln
subs: adj. (f. -sī), Like a corn-bin, rotund, big, fat, bulky, lubberly.

def: v.n. To groan, moan, grunt; to utter a forced sound (as in straining vehemently at lifting a weight, or at stool, &c.); to strain (at), to make strenuous efforts (syn. karāhnā, and kāṅkhnā).

ety: S. कुथ्+rā = Prk. अडओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. Putrefaction; impurity, uncleanness, foulness.
s.f. A small house, a cottage, a hut ( = kuṭir).
s.f. Innuendo; cutting speech; satire.
s.f. A box (gen. of wood, but sometimes of silver, in which precious stones, or electuaries, are kept); a snuff-box; a pill-box.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Crooked, bent; perverse, untoward; tortuous, disingenuous, dishonest, fraudulent; wicked, sinful; cruel, ruthless; — a disingenuous or dishonest man; a cruel man, &c.
s.f. = H کٿلپن कुटिलपन kuṭil-pan, s.m. = S کٿلتو कुटिलत्व kuṭilatva, s.m. Crookedness; perversity; tortuousness, disingenuousness, dishonesty, guile; — ruthlessness, cruelty.
s.m. = H کٿلپن कुटिलपन kuṭil-pan, s.m. = S کٿلتا कुटिलता kuṭilatā, vulg. kuṭiltā, s.f. Crookedness; perversity; tortuousness, disingenuousness, dishonesty, guile; — ruthlessness, cruelty.
s.m. = S کٿلتا कुटिलता kuṭilatā, vulg. kuṭiltā, s.f. = S کٿلتو कुटिलत्व kuṭilatva, s.m. Crookedness; perversity; tortuousness, disingenuousness, dishonesty, guile; — ruthlessness, cruelty.
s.m. A small house, a cottage, hut, hovel.
s.m. A small house, a cottage, a hut, hovel ( = kuṭir, and kuṭi).
s.m. = kuṭīr or kuṭir.

ety: fem. of kuttā, q.v.; — iyā = S. aff. इका
def: s.f. A bitch; a term of abuse
subs: s.m. A pup, puppy; — a bastard.

def: s.m. = کوتك kūtak, q.v.
adv. By dint of hammering and cutting.
s.f. A medicinal plant, black hellebore, Helleborus niger; wolf's bane; aconite; — a kind of grain-produce, Panicum miliare.
s.f. Cutting, dividing; — a nip; an incision (made with the teeth, &c., on thread, &c.; — a gnat (cf. S. kaṭu-kīṭa); — estrangement, separation (from), desertion (of)
subs: or kuṭkī lagānā (-meṅ), To nip; to make an incision (in), to cut (the string of a kite, &c.).

def: s.m. The sound of nibbling or munching; nibbling; — munch-munch.
s.m. An opening bud.
s.m.(?) Cotton.
v.n. To be estimated; to be appraised.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀺𑀢𑁆𑀢𑀺𑀬 (kittiya) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀦- (-na-), from Sanskrit *कियत्त (*kiyatta), variant of कियत् (kíyat). Doublet of کِتَّا (kittā). Cognate with Punjabi ਕਿਤਨਾ /کِتْنا (kitnā).

def: v.t. = kūṭnā, q.v.

ety: kūt˚, prob. = S. कुट्टय(ति), rt. कुट्ट्; cf. kūt, and kūtak
def: v.t. To appraise, value, estimate.

ety: kūṭ˚ = Prk. कुट्ट(इ) or कुट्टे(इ) = S. कुट्टय(ति), rt. कुट्ट्
def: v.t. To pound, beat, bruise, crush; macerate, to thresh (corn, &c.); to cudgel (syn. pīṭnā).
s.m. A pimp, a pander; a go-between; a mischief-maker.
s.f. Pimping; — the wages of a pimp.

ety: kuṭnā˚ = kuṭṭan, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि, caus. augment
def: v.t. To entice, seduce, inveigle, wheedle, coax.

ety: fr. kuṭnā+pā = Prk. प्पअं = S. त्व+कं
def: s.m. Pimping; — cucholdom
adj. Made of cotton.

ety: v.n. fr. قطر 'to tie in a line,' &c.
def: s.m. Diameter (of a circle); diagonal (of a square, &c.); cord (of an arc)

ety: prob. a var. form of katran
def: s.f. That which is gnawed or bitten off with the teeth, nibblings.
adv. See s.v. کتره kutra.
adj. & s.m. Cutting; gnawing, biting; cutter, gnawer, biter; — a puppy.
s.f. Reproach, censure; disrespect, contempt; calumny.
part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Reproached, blamed, censured; reviled, calumniated; despised, contemned; contemptible, despicable, low, vile; — a vile or contemptible man, &c.
subs: (˚ta+āć˚), s.m. Bad or reprehensible conduct, low behaviour, &c.
s.m. Low or vile language; slander.

ety: Prk. कुक्कुरो, or कुक्कुरु; S. कुक्कुरः
def: s.m. A dog; — the part of a gun-lock which is acted on by the trigger; a hand-vice; a trade-mark; — the ear of a kind of grass (syn. ćirćiṭā)
subs: s.m. Bite of a dog; one bitten by a dog
s.m. A toadstool, fungus (syn. sāṅp-kī ćhatrī).
ex: میرَا کُتَّا ہَمیشَہ بِلِّی پَر بَھوںکْتَا ہَےْ۔
merā kuttā hameśa billī par bha͠uktā hai.
My dog always barks at a cat.

def: s.m. A bird (esp. a pigeon) whose wings have been clipped.
s.m. pl. (of قاطع qāt̤ěʻ), Robbers; assassins
s.m. A pimp, a pander; a go-between; a mischief-maker.
s.f. A bawd, a procuress, a go-between
subs: s.m. The house of a procuress (where clandestine prostitution is carried on); a baudy-house, a brothel.

def: s.m. = kuṭnāpā, q.v.
s.m. A mountain: — sexual intercourse; pleasure; — exclusion; oneness.

ety: Prk. कुट्टिआ; S. कुट्टिता, rt. कुट्ट्
def: s.f. Chopped straw or fodder; pulverized paper, papier mache (used for binding books, making pen-boxes, stiffening caps, &c.); — cutting (a friend); alienation, estrangement
subs: s.m. One who chops up straw or fodder
To chop up straw or fodder; — to beat severely, make mince-meat (of)

ety: fem. of kuttā, q.v.; — iyā = S. aff. इका
def: s.f. A bitch; a term of abuse
subs: s.m. A pup, puppy; — a bastard.

def: s.m. A book-shop; a library; a book-shelf
s.m.f.(?), Anything interesting, fun, sport, pastime, festivity, entertainment; a surprising object, a show, spectacle; eagerness, desire, curiosity; vehemence, impetuosity, violence.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Sportive, fond of sport or festivity, highly interested in (any extraordinary matter), curious, inquisitive; eager, impatient, vehement, impetuous; — a curious or inquisitive person, &c.
s.m. Kith and kin, relatives, kindred, kinsfolk, household, family, progeny, race
s.m. = کٿمب kuṭumb, q.v.
s.m. Kith and kin, relatives, kindred, kinsfolk, household, family, progeny, race
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of or belonging to a household, or family, &c.; having a large family; — a householder, a paterfamilias; a member of a family; a kinsman.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Of or belonging to a household, or family, &c.; having a large family; — a householder, a paterfamilias; a member of a family; a kinsman.
adj. & s.m. = kuṭumbī, q.v.
s.m. A disease of the eyes in infants, weakness of the eyes.
s.m. contrac. of kotwāl, q.v.
s.f. = kotwālī, q.v.
v.t. To cause to be hammered, or pounded, or bruised, to have or get (a thing) pounded, &c.; — to submit to carnal intercourse.
s.m. The act of sitting; sitting down; remaining in one place.
s.f. pl. (of quwwat, q.v.), Powers, virtues, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Using bad language, scurrilous, abusive; censorious: detracting, slanderous.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Using bad or filthy language, &c.; — a person who uses filthy or abusive language; a calumniator.
s.m. Low or scurrilous language, abuse; evil-speaking, detraction, slander.
s.m. Bad or foul language; abuse; evil-speaking, calumny.
s.m. A water-lily; — the earth.
s.m. (f. -ī) = kuṅwārā, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Astringent in flavour.
s.m. 1˚ = kuṅwar, or kumār, q.v.; — 2˚ = kubaṛ, q.v.; — 3˚ = kuver, q.v.

r: kuwār, kuʼār, kūʼār, kuwārā, kuʼārā, kūʼārā
def: s.m. = kuṅwār, or kumār, q.v.

r: kuwārā, kuʼārā, kūʼārā, kuwār, kuʼār, kūʼār
def: s.m. = kuṅwār, or kumār, q.v.
s.f. = kuṅwārī, or kumārī, q.v.
s.f. Unseasonable or unsuitable time, inopportune moment
subs: adv. In season and out of season.

def: s.m. The god of riches, the Plutus of Hindū mythology
subs: s.m. The world or realm of Kuver, or the place where he keeps his treasures.

def: s.m. Erroneous or wrong decision, ill judgment, imprudence.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Ill-judging, indiscreet, imprudent; — an indiscreet person, an ill-advised person, &c.
adj. & s.m. = kūbṛā or kubṛā, q.v.
s.f. A bad course of conduct; bad character; bad state or condition.
s.m. = quwwat, q.v.

ety: for A. قوة, v.n. fr. قوي 'to be robust, or strong,' &c.
def: s.f. Strength, power, virtue, force, vigour, energy; authority; a faculty
subs: Power of attraction; allurement; power of absorption
Power of expulsion; repelling power; (in Mech.) force of repulsion
The faculty or power of anger, irascible power; rage
or quwwat-ě-ḵẖayāl, The power of fancy; the thinking faculty; reason
The power of apprehension or comprehension; the intellectual faculty, intellect
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُوت (qūt), from Arabic قُوت (qūt).

def: s.m. = kūʼāṅ, q.v.
s.m. = kūʼāṅ, q.v.
s.m. A crooked back, a hump
s.m. An earthen water-bottle with a long narrow neck, a gugglet; a pitcher; a jug; a jar; — sugar crystallized in an earthen pot or pan; — a kind of rose
subs: 'A river (or the sea) in a jug'; much in little, multum in parvo.

ety: for A. قضاة, pl. of qaẓi
def: s.m. Judges, justices.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُضَات (quzāt), from Arabic قُضَاة (quḍāh).

def: s.m. A decoction, an infusion.

ety: for kiʼā = kihā = Ap. Prk. केहो = Prk. केद्दहो = S.कीट्टशः
def: pron. (obl. kāhe), What? how? why? (The form is the same in both numbers, e.g. kyā hai, 'What is it?'; — kyā haiṅ, 'what are they?'; kyā is often the sign of interrogation at the beginning of a sentence, like the Persian āyā and the Latin an or num, and is, like them, untranslated when in such a position); — conj. Whether, or; — intj. What! how!
subs: How fine! well done! bravo! &c. (see kyā-ḵẖūb): kyā bāt hai, It is a matter of no significance or importance; — it goes without saying, what need to mention it, not a doubt of it; — well done! bravo!
or kyā jāniye, What does one know? who knows? perhaps
or kyā ḵẖāk hai, What great thing is it? it's a mere nothing, or a mere trifle
How excellent! how fine! bravo! well done! what a joke! what next!
or kyā muẓāyaqa, What matter! never mind! there is no harm
What all? what not? something extraordinary
kyā — ki? utrum — an? is this the case or that?
(e.g. kyā īśvar patthar hai ki ātmā, 'is God a stone or (is He not rather) a spirit?)
What things? what things severally or respectively; what not; all sorts of
What a strange thing has come to light!
kyā maqdūr, kyā imkān, kyā tāb, or kyā iḵẖtiyār hai, What power has he? what can he do, or what dare he do? he dare not
What face has he? with what face?
What name? what do you call it?
What has happened? what is the matter?; — what has become of it?
How very! (e.g. kyā-hī ḵẖūb, 'how very fine!'); how very beautifully! (e.g. kyā-hī barse thī nūr-kī jhaṛī)
What is it? what is the matter? what do you say?

def: (i.q. -ke hāṅ), postp. (dialec.), At the house or abode of; of; with; in the neighbourhood of, near.

ety: prob. Prk. किणो; S. किं; or fr. किं with aff. मत्, Prk. मंतो
def: adv. Why? wherefore? how? what? well? then, because
subs: or kyon-kar-ke, or kyoṅ-ke, adv. By what means? in what way? how? in what manner? why? — conj. Because, &c. ( = next, q.v.)
conj. Because, because that, since; in that, inasmuch as
Why should it not be? why not? undoubtedly, assuredly

def: s.f. contrac. of lākshā, q.v.; — past conj. part. of lānā, q.v. ( = lāke).

r: lā, un, in, ir, im, less, lā-ubālī, lāʼo-bālī, lā ubāl
ety: it occurs in combination with Arabic or Persian words, and may sometimes be rendered by the English neg. prefixes un-, in-, ir-, im-, or the affix less
def: adj. Fearing nothing, fearless; insolent; careless, thoughtless, indifferent; licentious; petulant; — s.f. Fearlessness, carelessness; thoughtlessness, indifference
subs: s.m. An unbeliever; a sceptic; an irreligious person, one who does not observe the religious precepts of Islāmism
vulg. lā-bud (lit. 'there is no separating oneself' from a thing, or 'there is no avoiding' it), adv. It cannot be but; inevitably, necessarily, infallibly, assuredly
adj. Unavoidable, inevitable, certain; necessary; essential; positive; — involuntary; compulsory
adj. Without a second, incomparable, unequalled, unparalleled
(lit. 'there is no avoiding it'), adv. Necessarily, of necessity, indispensably; undoubtedly, by all means; of course, as a matter of course; consequently
adj. Incapable of answering, silenced, confuted, speechless, answerless, disconcerted; beyond all question; unequalled, matchless

adj. & adv. Without remedy, helpless, powerless; disabled, disarmed ( = nā-ćār); destitute, forlorn, poor; — without excuse; at a loss; without an alternative, having no alternative; under constraint, under pressure of necessity, perforce; compelled, forced; — lā-ćār karnā (-ko), To render helpless or powerless; to disarm; to incapacitate; to leave no alternative to, to put pressure upon, to compel, force, oblige
or lā-ćārī, s.f. Helplessness; inability, disability, incapacity; forlornness, destitution, want of means, poverty
adj. Unproductive, fruitless, barren; unprofitable, profitless, bootless; abortive; need less, unavailing
vulg. lā-ḥal, adj. Not to be untied, not to be solved; indissoluble; difficult, abstruse

or lā ḥaula wa lā quwwata illā biʼl-lāhi, 'There is no strength nor power but in (or by means of) God'; there is no striving against fate (a deprecatory formula which Muslims repeat on the receipt of bad news, or on hearing of (or seeing) anything disagreeable, or to express aversion or contempt, or to drive away evil spirits)
or lā-ḥaul bhejnā, To repeat the deprecatory formula lā-haul, &c.
vulg. lā-ḵẖirāj, adj. Rent-free (lands), allodial; — s.f. Rent-free land
s.m. A house built on rent-free land
s.m. A holder of rent-free land
s.f. Rent-free land; land exempted from payment of revenue
adj. & s.m. Without claim or demand; — a release, an acquittance, a deed or act of relinquishment; a withdrawal of claim; a deed of surrender, a deed foregoing a claim, or admitting there is none
adj. Without cure or remedy, incurable, remediless, irremediable
adj. & adv. Undoubted; infallible; unquestionable; — no doubt, undoubtedly, unquestionably, infallibly, certainly
adj. Silent, speechless; — s.f. Improper speech, abuse ( = lā-suḵẖan)
adj. Speechless, silent, taciturn; — s.m. Improper talk, harsh words, railing, abuse
adj. & s.m. Without any associate or partner; — one who is without an associate, &c.
vulg. lā-shak, adj. & adv. Undoubted, indubitable; — without doubt, doubtless
adj. Useless, unprofitable; vain, absurd, groundless
adj. Incurable, without remedy, remediless, hopeless
adj. Without knowledge or information, unaware, unknowing, ignorant
s.f. Want or absence of information; ignorance

adj. & adv. Without a word, speechless, taciturn; indisputable, undisputed, unquestionable; unconditional, fixed; — indisputably, no question; without fail, positively, absolutely, certainly, undoubtedly, assuredly; — let it pass, agreed, no more to be said
adj. Inexistent, with no abode, without a dwelling-place; — s.m. The Deity
adj. Having no end or limit, boundless, infinite
adj. Heirless, having or leaving no heir; to which there is no heir (property), unclaimed
adj. = lā-wāris̤, q.v.; — s.f. Property to which there is no heir, unclaimed property ( = lā-wāris̤ī māl)
adj. Not allowed, prohibited, illicit, unlawful
adj. That ceases not, eternal, durable, unfading
adj. & s.m. Knowing nothing, ignorant; — an ignorant fellow, a blockhead
adj. Absurd, unmeaning, insignificant; obscene, abusive

def: s.m. pl. (of lūlū), Pearls.
s.m. Playing; play, sport, game
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لعب (la'b), from Arabic لَعْب (laʕb).

def: s.m. corr. of lābh, q.v.
adj. Lip to lip; — brim ul (syn. lab-rez)
subs: s.f. State of having the lips closed

(or labeṅ) band hona, v.n. The lips to be (or to remain) closed; to be silent; the lips not to open, or to be taken off (from a thing luscious to the taste; e.g. ambe aise shīrīn the ki labeṅ band hotī thīṅ); to be cloyed (with sweets)
adj. & s.m. Biting the lip; fastidious; fretful; — one who bites his lips; a fastidious man; a prig; a beau; — lab-ḵẖā paḵẖnā, s.m. A beau
s.m. Bank of a river; sea-shore
adj. Brimful, overflowing, running over, exuberant
or lab hilānā, To open (or to move) the lips, to speak
(-meṅ), To apply (or put) the lip (to); — to apply saliva (to)
(or dam) honā, The life (or the breath) to be on the lips, to be dying
To shave or clip the moustache.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَب (lab). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi لب (lb /⁠lab⁠/).

def: s.m. Request, desire; supplication, prayer; — ridicule, irony; a jest, joke, anything facetious; — adulation, flattery, coaxing.

ety: for A. لبادة lubādat, or lubbādat; cf. labbād
def: s.m. A cloak for rain; a quilted cloak; wrapper; great-coat; pelisse.
adj. See s.v. lab.
s.f. The pot in which palm-juice (tāṛī) is collected from the tree.
adj. & s.m. Lying, mendacious, false, untrue, unreal; — a prater, talker, babbler; liar; a false person; a rogue, rascal; a plunderer; — (in Dakh.) s.f. = labāṛī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Lying, mendacious, false, untrue, unreal; — a prater, talker, babbler; liar; a false person; a rogue, rascal; a plunderer; — (in Dakh.) s.f. = labāṛī, q.v.

ety: i.q. labāṛ, q.v., for which it is used in comp.
def: s.f. Giving a dry breast to a child
subs: or labaṛ-sabaṛ, s.m. Tittle-tattle, gossip, nonensical talk
s.m., labaṛ-ḵẖandī, s.f., A lying, false man; a naughty, mischievous child; — a lying, bad woman
s.f. A row, a riot; — a mess, scrape; — cheating, unfairness (in a game, &c.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Fastidious; fretful, touchy; — a fastidious person, &c.

ety: i.q. labāṛ, q.v., for which it is used in comp.
def: s.f. Giving a dry breast to a child
subs: or labaṛ-sabaṛ, s.m. Tittle-tattle, gossip, nonensical talk
s.m., labaṛ-ḵẖandī, s.f., A lying, false man; a naughty, mischievous child; — a lying, bad woman
s.f. A row, a riot; — a mess, scrape; — cheating, unfairness (in a game, &c.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Fastidious; fretful, touchy; — a fastidious person, &c.

ety: labāṛ, q.v. + Prk. इआ = S. इका
def: s.f. Falsehood, untruth, duplicity, dishonesty, knavery; a lie, fabrication, forgery, fraud; plundering, plunder; — (dialec.) meanness; silliness.
adj. & s.m. = labāṛ, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = labāṛ, q.v.
v.t. (Dakh.) To plunder, spoil (syn. lūṭnā, q.v.; cf. labaṛnā).
v.n. To speak or act falsely or deceitfully, to tell lies, to fabricate; to act absurdly, improperly, or foolishly.
s.f. A plaything; a doll, a puppet, a marionette
subs: s.m. A player of puppets; a player; buffoon; punchinello
s.f. Play, amusement; puppet-playing; theatrical representation.

def: s.m. A felt-manufacturer; felt-vendor.
adv. I am standing in thy service; I await thy commands; here I am; what dost thou want with me? what wouldst thou have?
s.m. Mud; alluvial deposit; thick mud formed by water flooding a stiff soil.
s.f. A woman whose husband (or lover) is faithless.
part. (f. -a), Taken, seized, taken hold of; obtained, received; got, gained, won, acquired, attained; recovered
subs: (S. labdha-praṇāśa), s.m. The loss of anything acquired; — one who has obtained the fulfilment of a promise or a vow
adj. & s.m. Restored to (one's) senses, come to (one's) right mind; recovered
s.m. (f. -ī), A slave received as a gift or transfer.

def: s.f. Obtaining, gaining; acquisition, acquirement, attainment; gain, advantage, profit; (in Arith.) the quotient.
intj. Yes, Sir! hey!
s.m. A stick, club, cudgel; — (dialec.) family, followers, cattle, baggage, conveniences (as attending a person on a journey).
adj. Possessed of a lip or lips; having a rim or margin.

ety: lab = lap (fr. S. लिप्)+erā = Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: s.m. The fruit Cordia myxa (syn. lasoṛā).

s.m. Getting, gaining, obtaining, acquiring; — acquirement, acquisition, gain, profit, advantage; emolument; welfare; produce; interest; — pickings, perquisites; — name of the eleventh astrological house or lunar mansion
subs: v.n. To profit or benefit (by, -se), to make gain or profit (from); to make a profit (of, -kā), to gain, earn
or lābh-dāʼī, or lābh-dāyak, or lābh-kārī, or lābh-kar, adj. Causing gain, making or procuring profit; affording advantage, profitable; advantageous, lucrative
adj. (f. -kī), Gainful, profitable, advantageous, beneficial, of use
v.t. To gain, acquire, win; to attain
adj. (f. -ī), Abounding in profit or advantage, profitable, advantageous, lucrative
(lābha+alābha), s.m. Profit and loss, advantage and disadvantage, gain and detriment.

def: adj. (f. -ā), To be found or met with; capable of being acquired, acquirable, obtainable, procurable, attainable; — suitable, proper, fit; — s.m. Gain, profit ( = lābh); interest (on money).
s.f. The juice (the produce) of the sugar-cane when boiling (or boiled) into sugar (syn. rāb, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. See s.v. lab.

ety: for lib-lib, by redupl. of S. लिप, or by met. fr. pilpil; — cf. liblibā
def: s.m. Stickiness, clamminess, viscosity, glutinousness, tenacity (syn. las or lassā); — the gizzard (syn. pathrī).
adj. Naked; shapeless.
s.f. See s.v. lab.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Talkative; false, &c. ( = labāṛ, q.v.); — left-handed; awkward.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Talkative; false, &c. ( = labāṛ, q.v.); — left-handed; awkward.
adj. & s.f. Talkative; false, &c. (woman); — a talkative woman; a false woman, &c. (see labāṛ).

ety: prob. fr. S. लस+कृ; see laćnā
def: s.f. Bending, bending down; flexibility; spring, springiness, elasticity; — a strain, a sprain; — softness, mildness, delicacy; a liking or taste (as contracted for a thing)

ety: laćak, q.v.+nā = anā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.n. To be bent (as the bough of a tree, &c.); to bend, to yield; to spring, to start; — to receive a sprain or strain, to be sprained
part. adj. (f. -ī), Flexible, springy, elastic.
v.t. To cause to bend, to bend, bow, crook; to deflect.
s.m. Bending; deflexion; flexibility; elasticity.
adj. See s.v. lā.

ety: rt. of laćnā+ar = Prk. अडो = S. रः
def: adj. & s.m. Yielding, pliant, soft; weak, feeble, lacking back-bone; loose, shaky, wanting force or vigour (as language, &c., e.g. laćar ʻibārat); — a soft or yielding person, a weak man; a foolish fellow, simpleton, noodle.
adj. corr. of لاچار lā-ćār, q.v.s.v. lā.
s.m. corr. of لچها laććhā, q.v.

ety: prob. S. यष्टिकः; cf. larćhā, and laṛī
def: s.m. (f. & dim., -ī), A bundle (of thread, &c.), a skein; a necklace of silk or beads (of glass or gold, fitting close to the neck); a bracelet (or an anklet) of two or more rows of chain-work; a tassel; — a thin shred or slice (as of ginger, or onion, &c.)
subs: adj. Shred-like; entangled, involved, confused, jumbled; vague, desultory, roundabout; forming a string or chain, serial, consecutive (as words or speech).

def: s.m. Distinctive mark, mark, sign, token, symbol, indication, characteristic, feature; description, definition; beauty; auspiciousness; trait, habit, manner, fashion, custom; state, condition (for other meanings see lakshaṅ)
subs: or laććhan sīkhnā, To learn mauners
(-kī), 'The beauty (of a person) to fade,' to lose (one's) beauty; to be or become disfigured; — to degenerate, deteriorate; to become depraved, or demoralized, or corrupted.

def: s.m. A kind of grain, Cynosurus coracanus (called, by Europeans, 'Natchenny').
s.m. (dialec.) corr. of laksh, q.v.; also = lakshaṅ, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = lakshmī, q.v.
s.f. Blood and water mixing.

ety: laććhī, fem. of laććhā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आवे = S. आपि caus. augment
def: To make skeins (of thread, &c.); to reel (thread, &c.).
s.m. = lakshmaṅ, q.v.
s.f. corr. of lakshmī, q.v.
s.f. corr. of ilāćī, or elāćī, q.v.

ety: lać˚, rt. of laćnā+Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Bending easily, limber, springy, flexible, elastic.
s.m. A jolt, shove, push; a strain, a sprain; — a liking or taste (for); — gram pottage, &c. (see lāsā); — a barge, a pleasure boat; — gold or silver lace.
part. Bent; sprung, started; strained, &c.
v.t. To cause to bend; to jolt; to strain, to sprain.

ety: by redupl. of lać, rt. of laćnā, q.v.
def: s.f. The sound made by the play of a flexible (or an elastic) body (as a switch, or cane, &c.).
v.n. To be sticky, or clammy, or viscous or glutinous (cf. lajlajā); to be flexible or elastic.

ety: lać˚ = S. लस्; for change of s to ć, cf. lālać
def: v.n. To be or become bent or bowed; to bend, bow, yield, give way, sink in, sag (as a wall, or beam, &c.)
s.m. = لاڙ lāṛ, q.v.
s.f. Load, burden, freight; — entrail, bowel

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Beloved, pet, darling; tenderly nurtured; — a beloved object, a pet; a favourite; a lover, sweetheart; a bridegroom; — a character in a play; — raw indigo-plant (as it comes into the factory)
subs: Lover and beloved, lovers; bridegroom and bride.

def: s.m. Ladanum, a resinous substance exuding from the leaves and branchlets of the Cistus creticus.
v.t. 'To cause to be loaded,' to load, lade, freight
subs: v.t. To load; to help in loading.

def: s.m. A load, burden; lading, cargo; — an arched roof or terrace.
adj. Loaded with (or full of) patches; clumsily patched.
part. (old H.) = liyā, p.p. of lenā, q.v.

s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (made of the meal of chick-pea, or mūṅg, &c., with the addition of sugar and ghī, and sometimes rasped cocoa-nut kernel, and pistachio nuts, and formed into the shape of large boluses or balls); a ball, lump, round mass; (fig.) benefit, gain; stock, capital, principal
subs: (-meṅ), To distribute laḍḍūs (among); to celebrate a piece of good fortune
or laḍḍū banānā (-kā), To make laḍḍūs or balls (of), to form into round lumps
(-ko), To feast (one) with laḍḍūs; to bribe, to grease the palms of
v.n. To find something good; to gain, profit

To eat a Thag's laḍḍū and be done for (an allusion taken from ṭhags, or other sharpers, who join a party of travellers, and, having got into their confidence, entertain them with sweetmeats impregnated with Datura or other intoxicating drug, that they may rob them with impunity while under its stupefying influence); to be or become intoxicated, stupefied, distracted, or foolish
or khānā, or luṛhānā, To break, or to eat, or to roll, imaginary laḍḍūs; to build castles in the air, to form vain fancies.

def: adj. & s.m. = lādū, q.v.
s.m. = laḍḍū, q.v.
s.m. = لڙها laṛhā, q v.
s.f. Lading; a small load (such as is put on an ox, or an ass, &c.).
adj. (f. ladnī), For burthen; sumpter.

ety: lād˚, rt. of lādnā+iyā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: s.m. One who loads; a loader.
adj. & s.m. = lāṛlā, q.v.

ety: lād˚ = Prk. लद्धो = S. लब्धः, p.p.p. of rt. लभ्
def: v.t. To load, lade, burden, freight (an animal, or a cart, or a boat or ship); to take in cargo; — to pile or heap (upon, -par); to place heavy (or many) things (upon); to oppress (one) with tasks or charges; to lay on thickly; (in wrestling) to have (one) on the cross-buttock
v.n. To be loaded or laden; to be borne or carried
subs: v.t. To load oneself; to load, burden; to mount (upon, -par).

def: adj. f. of ladī, q.v.
s.m. A carrier.
adj. & s.f. Beloved (wife or daughter); — a pet, &c. ( = lāḍā, q.v.)
s.m. Packing and loading; — a sailor's knot.

s.m.f. For loading; fit to carry a load; — a burden-bearer, a beast of burden (as a pony, or ass, &c.): -lādū karnā (-ko), To mount (or to load) a horse or colt for the first time; to convert (an animal) into a beast of burden.
s.m. (dialec. or rustic) = laḍḍū, q.v.
s.m. (rustic) = laḍḍū, q.v.
adj. & s.m. = lādū, q.v.

ety: rel. n. fr. the prep. ladun, 'near, by, with, in the possession of'
def: adj. Inspired; infused (as knowledge).
v.t. To cause to be loaded; to cause to load ( = ladānā)
s.f. (dialec.) = lāhe, q.v.

ety: for lāp; S. लाप, rt. लप्
def: s.f. Self-praise; boasting, bragging, boast, brag
subs: s.m. A boaster, braggart; a blusterer
v.n. To boast, &c. ( = lāf-zanī karnā, q.v.).

def: adj. & s.m. Addicted to boasting, or vapouring, or vainglorious talk; — ready with plausible excuses or explanations; — making extravagant and empty promises and professions; — a vainglorious fellow, a vapourer, a braggart; a loose character (the word is often darkly understood and indefinitely applied).

adj. & s.m. Addicted to boasting, or vapouring, or vainglorious talk; — ready with plausible excuses or explanations; — making extravagant and empty promises and professions; — a vainglorious fellow, a vapourer, a braggart; a loose character (the word is often darkly understood and indefinitely applied).

ety: inf. n. of لفّ 'to fold, involve,' &c.
def: s.m. Involving, involution, folding, wrapping; complicating, complication; twisting; joining, adding (one thing to another); assembling, collecting; mixing, mingling
subs: s.m. (in Rhet.) 'Collecting and scattering'; a figure corresponding to the Chiasmus of the classics. (The following are examples from Persian poetry: Ćūn jūd o jalāl o hunar o tabʻ o k ffi ū, Abr o falak o aḵẖtar o daryā mat̤ar nest, 'As (are) his generosity, majesty, skill, character, and liberal hand, The clouds, the firmament, the stars, the ocean, and the rain are not'; Jān o dil-ě walī o ʻadawī-ě tu roz-o-shab, Az waʻda-o waʻīd-ě-tu pur nūr o nār bād, 'May the soul and the heart of thy friend and of thy foe be ever, Full of light or of fire by thy promise or thy threat.' Examples from English poety are: 'Annual for me the grape, the rose renew, The juice nectarious, and the balmy dew' (Pope); 'An oven that is stopped or river stayʼd, Burneth more hotly, swelleth with more rage' (Shakespeare).)

def: adj. & s.m. Eloquent, voluble, wordy, verbose, loquacious, garrulous; — an eloquent man, &c.; a pedant.
s.f. Eloquence; volubility, verbosity, loquacity; pedantry.
adj. Wrapped, folded, involute; complex; collected promiscuously from all parts; — s.m. A mixed multitude, a crowd; a congregation of a number of various tribes; — a friend, an acquaintance.
s.m. Lieutenant.

ety: inf. n. of لفظ 'to eject' (from the mouth
def: s.m. A word, vocable; a saying; a term; — pronunciation
subs: adv. Word by (or for) word; verbatim.
ex: مَیں دو لَفْظوں میں یہ بَیاں نَہِیں کَر سَکْتا۔
ma͠i do lafzõ mẽ ye bayā̃ nahī̃ kar saktā.
I cannot express this in only two words.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian لَفْظ (lafz), from Arabic لَفْظ (lafẓ).

def: adv. Verbally, literally; explicitly, expressly; distinctly pronounced.
adj. Relating to the pronunciation, or to the word; enunciative; verbal; grammatical; literal.

ety: Prk. लग्ग, or लग्गा; see lāgnā, and lagnā
def: s.f. Harmonious relation; affinity; correlation; bearing; appositeness, adaptedness (of time, or place, or of means with an end, or of appearances with a fact or truth, &c.); relevancy; consistency, concurrence, correspondence, reciprocal suitableness or agreeableness; (in Math.) ratio; — attachment, affection, love; — an application, or a direction (of the mind), aiming; aim; attention; exertion, endeavour, attempt; — touching, reaching, attaining (to), approach; cost, expenditure; — hitting, striking; fixing; — an attack of ill-fortune, a calamitous occurrence, a blow, stroke; enmity, animosity, hostility, rancour, spite, grudge; rivalry, competition; — narcotic quality (of a substance); — intrigue, plot; a secret; — trick, legerdemain, sleight of hand, jugglery; a charm, spell, fascination; — catch, hold, support, basis, ground; a prop
subs: (-se), To bear enmity (against), to show hostility (to), to become an enemy

(-se), To be related (to), to belong (to), pertain (to); to relate (to), to have reference (to), to have to do (with); to have an interest (in); to be intent (on); — to harbour animosity or ill-feeling (against), to have a grudge (against)
(-ko), To cause (a person) to eat anything charmed; — to charm, to cast a spell upon
s.f. Attachment, fondness; partiality, favouritism (syn. t̤araf-dārī); — reserve; — deceit
(-kī), To be enamoured (of), to fall in love (with)

adj. & adv. Without connection or tie, unconnected, disconnected, detached, separate; unfettered, free; — impartial, neutral; disinterested; unconcerned, indifferent; — unaided, unsupported; — without let or hindrance; without fail; — genuine, pure, unmixed; without enmity or spite; without any trickery; frank, candid, sincere; — without reserve; frankly; impartially, fairly, &c.

ety: Prk. लग्गि or लग्गे = S. लग्ने, loc. of lagna, rt. लग्
def: prep. To, up to, as far as; close to, near, till, until; whilst, during
subs: adj. & adv. Close, near, approximate; — close upon, nearly, approximately, about, almost; in the proximity (of, -ke)
adv. (dialec.) In close contact; close together; without intermission, uninterruptedly, incessantly.

ety: Prk. लगिअ; S. लगित्वा, rt. लग्
def: part. Having joined; having attached oneself (to), &c.; — adv. (in comp.) In close contact, closely; in company with, along with, with
subs: v.n. To follow closely; to go with, to accompany; to court friendship (with, -se, or -ke sāth); to become familiar or intimate (with)

v.n. To continue joined or attached (to, -se); to continue fixed; to continue without interruption; to stick (to, -meṅ, or -se); to persevere (in); to be ready fixed, or attached, or put up, &c. (see lagā rahnā, s.v. lagā)
adv. With united force; all together.

ety: perf. part. of lagnā, and = Prk. लग्गिअओ or लग्गिदओ; S. लगित+कः, rt. लग्
def: part. (f. -ī), Attached (to, -se, or -meṅ), connected (with), joined (to); stuck; — engaged or occupied (in, -meṅ), busy (with); — scorched, burnt; blasted; accursed
subs: or lage-tār, adj. & adv. Close together; uninterrupted, successive, continuous, incessant; — uninterruptedly, continually, constantly; ad seriatim
v.n. To go along continually or uninterruptedly, to keep going; to follow
v.n. To go along (with, -ke sāth), to accompany

(-se), To continue in close contact (with); to continue (in or at) without interruption; to be kept on (in an office, &c.); — to stick (to), to persevere (in); to prosecute, to pursue; to be busy (with), be engaged or occupied (in); — to lie in wait, or in ambush

adj. (f. lagī-lipṭī), In close connection; entwined (round, -meṅ), clasping; involved; obscure, indistinct; partial, biassed, prejudiced; — s.m. A small piece or remnant; — lagī-lipṭī rakhnā or kahnā (-se), To show (or speak with) partiality; — to speak in involved sentences, to speak obscurely, or dubiously, or with mental reserve; to hum and haw
adj. (f. -ī), Attached, &c. ( = lagā); adjoining, adjacent contiguous; — entangled, involved (in)
adv. About (one), with; — lage-lage, adv. In close connection (with), side by side (with, -ke), collaterally, along (with)
adv. While the hand is in; whilst (you, &c. are) about it; at the moment of (his) doing it; concurrently; immediately upon, just upon or after, in flagrante delicto.

def: prep. Beginning (with), from; inclusive (of), &c. ( = lagā-ke, and le-kar).

ety: lagā˚, rt. of lagānā+āʼī = awāʼī = Prk. एअव्वअ+इआ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+ता+इका
def: s.f. Applying, application, &c. (see lagānā); — imputation, false accusation; — assessment (on land); rent, revenne (charged on a field or estate; — syn. lagān)
subs: s.f. Causing a quarrel and then making peace (between); sowing dissension; — a make-bait.

def: s.f. = لگام lag̠ām, q.v.
s.m. Bridle; bit; bit and reins together
subs: (-ko), lagām ćaṛhānā (-par), or lagām lagānā (-ko), To put a bridle (on), to bridle; to curb, check, control
v.n. To be without bit or bridle, to be unbridled; to be free from check or restraint; to run riot
adj. Obedient to the rein (a horse).

def: s.m. Imputation, allegation, false accusation or charge; — assessment (on land); rent or revenue (from land); — putting to, stopping, making fast, mooring (a boat); place for making fast (a boat), landing-place; anchorage
s.m. A brazen or copper pan (in which the hands are washed, or in which bread, &c. is baked); a copper kneading-trough.
ex: مُسَلْمان، ہِنْدُوون کے ساتھ دُرْگاپُوجا میں شَرِیک ہوتے تہے اَور شادی بْیاہ وَغَیرَہ کے مَوقَع پَر بَرَہْمَنوں سے شُبھ لَگَن اَور سَعدِ ساعَت دَریافْت کِیا کَرتے تھے.
profesar 'azīz ahmad
Islamic Culture in the Indian Subcontinent

def: s.m. = lagna, q.v.
ex: مُسَلْمان، ہِنْدُوون کے ساتھ دُرْگاپُوجا میں شَرِیک ہوتے تہے اَور شادی بْیاہ وَغَیرَہ کے مَوقَع پَر بَرَہْمَنوں سے شُبھ لَگَن اَور سَعدِ ساعَت دَریافْت کِیا کَرتے تھے.

def: prep. (dialec.) = lag, q.v.
ex: مُسَلْمان، ہِنْدُوون کے ساتھ دُرْگاپُوجا میں شَرِیک ہوتے تہے اَور شادی بْیاہ وَغَیرَہ کے مَوقَع پَر بَرَہْمَنوں سے شُبھ لَگَن اَور سَعدِ ساعَت دَریافْت کِیا کَرتے تھے.

def: (i.q. lagnā, q.v.), s.m. & f. The being attached (to); the coming in contact (with); attachment, affection, love, friendship; desire, longing
subs: (-kī), To have an attachment (to), feel a passion (for), be in love (with).
ex: مُسَلْمان، ہِنْدُوون کے ساتھ دُرْگاپُوجا میں شَرِیک ہوتے تہے اَور شادی بْیاہ وَغَیرَہ کے مَوقَع پَر بَرَہْمَنوں سے شُبھ لَگَن اَور سَعدِ ساعَت دَریافْت کِیا کَرتے تھے.
